title: Gochujang Vodka Rigatoni
channel: Joshua Weissman — 3 Lazy Pasta Recipes (2 of 3)

ingredients.note: Weight not stated in the video — a standard 1 lb / 500 g box works. (10-minute cook time.)
Fermented chili paste — adds umami, sweetness and heat (no extra chilies needed).
Optional — he says it can be left out.
This is what creates the emulsion.
Added in two stages: ½ cup, then the remaining ¼ cup.

steps.text: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and season generously with salt. Add the rigatoni (about a 10-minute cook). Wait 3–4 minutes before starting the sauce.

Heat ¼ cup extra virgin olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the diced shallot and 3–4 sliced garlic cloves and season with salt. Sweat from cold for 1–2 minutes until just softened — don't let it take on color.

Add 3 Tbsp gochujang and ½ cup tomato paste. Stir and cook down over medium heat, stirring, until it starts frying in the oil and caramelizing (it will begin to stick a little — that's your cue to move on).
Add 1 Tbsp vodka (optional), then ¼ cup water. Stir together.
Add 1½ cups heavy cream and stir until evenly combined and emulsified (whisk it if you want to speed it up).
When the rigatoni is al dente (pull it 30–60 seconds early), transfer it into the sauce.
Cut the heat. Add 1–2 Tbsp cold cubed unsalted butter and ½ cup pecorino, and toss vigorously to emulsify into a glossy sauce.
Add the remaining ¼ cup grated pecorino romano and keep tossing until silky smooth with no cheese lumps. Splash in 1–2 Tbsp pasta water if it needs help.
Taste and season with salt. Give it one last toss, then serve with a little parmigiano or pecorino.
